extraction.one_line_summary: PHILIPSEN Jasper won the sprint after ALPECIN controlled the final turns, though commissaires reviewed potential irregular sprinting before confirming the victory.
tactical_patterns: POWLESS Neilson traded KOM points for sprint points with PICHON Laurent, showing calculated jersey defense over stage win ambitions.
ALPECIN executed lead-out through technical final corners with VAN DER POEL Mathieu, exploiting late positioning advantage over SOUDAL's earlier commitment.
CAVENDISH Mark positioned poorly mid-pack with only BOASSON HAGEN Edvald ahead at 2km, revealing ASTANA lead-out dysfunction on technical finales.
Commissaires delayed podium pending sprint irregularity review, suggesting PHILIPSEN Jasper rode borderline legal closing maneuver under pressure from VAN AERT Wout.

rider_signals.PHILIPSEN Jasper: positive: won third of last four TDF sprint stages, demonstrating consistent top-tier finishing power
rider_signals.VAN DER POEL Mathieu: positive: delivered PHILIPSEN through final technical corners despite sprinting fatigue, showing tactical versatility beyond classics strength
rider_signals.CAVENDISH Mark: negative: stranded mid-peloton at 2km despite BOASSON HAGEN Edvald forward, suggesting team unable to navigate technical run-in
rider_signals.POWLESS Neilson: neutral: prioritized KOM consolidation through point-trading rather than breakaway victory, tactical but predictable
rider_signals.SIMMONS Quinn: neutral: active in green jersey positioning for PEDERSEN Mads, indicating TREK sprint train development
rider_signals.YATES Adam: neutral: retained yellow for third consecutive day without GC stress, benefiting from flat parcours selection
